CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-24-2023 Set For Trial On Jun-03-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-06-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS NETWISE DATA, LLC, DUN & BRADSTREET, INC. DEMURRER to COMPLAINT

Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
David W. Garland, Esq. of EPSTEIN BECKER & GREEN, P.C., Tel:310.556.8861, for defendants and moving party;
Willliam S. Waldo, Esq. of BONONI LAW GROUP, LLP, Tel: 213.553.9200, plaintiff and opposing party;
Irene Gharapet, Esq. of LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP, Tel: 213.250.1800, for defendant, LIVERAMP, INC.

The matter was argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:

Netwise Data, LLC and Dun & Bradstreet, Inc.'s demurrer is sustained in part and overruled in part. Dun & Bradstreet is nowhere mentioned in the complaint's "factual allegations," so the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to D & B. (Id. at 2:23-6:8.)

As to Netwise, plaintiff pleads that its founder and CEO Dwight Gorall sexually assaulted and harassed her at a business event put on by plaintiff and her employer LiveRamp, Inc. (Cmplt. 3:6-4:9.)

Civil Code 51.9 (sexual harassment): Plaintiff pleads that she helped host the LiveRamp "executive dinner for its clients and business partners" such as Gorall's Netwise at which Gorall groped and otherwise sexually harassed her. (Cmplt. 3:6-12, 5:10-16, 14:3-10.) This suffices to plead that plaintiff had a business, service or professional relationship with Netwise. (Civil Code 51.9; CACI 3065(1).) Further, it is pled that Netwise knew of the sexual harassment because its founder and CEO Gorall conducted, and was told by plaintiff to stop, the harassment (allegations that must be accepted as true at the pleading stage). (Cmplt. 3:18-4:3.) The demurrer is overruled.

Aiding and Abetting (Govt. Code 12940(i)): The complaint adequately pleads FEHA aiding and abetting by Netwise. (Id. at 16:8-18:20.) Plaintiff pleads that she notified Netwise's Gorall immediately to "STOP" his harassment; more notification of Netwise was not needed. (Id. at 3:26-4:3.) The demurrer is overruled.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Julius Balentine, CSR#13859, Email: juliusbalentinecsr@gmail.com, Tel:714.471.2597. (302/RBU)

Defendants Netwise Data, Llc'S And Dun Bradstreet, Inc.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike
Off Calendar Feb-06-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS NETWISE DATA, LLC, DUN & BRADSTREET, INC.'s Motion To Strike

Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
David W. Garland, Esq. of EPSTEIN BECKER & GREEN, P.C., Tel:310.556.8861, for defendants and moving party;
Willliam S. Waldo, Esq. of BONONI LAW GROUP, LLP, Tel: 213.553.9200, plaintiff and opposing party;
Irene Gharapet, Esq. of LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP, Tel: 213.250.1800, for defendant LIVERAMP, INC.

The matter was argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:

Netwise Data, LLC and Dun & Bradstreet, Inc.'s motion to strike is granted in part and denied in part.

Dun & Bradstreet is nowhere mentioned in the complaint's "factual allegations," so the motion to strike is granted with leave to amend as to D & B. (Id. at 2:23-6:8.)

As to Netwise, "[f]or corporate punitive damages liability, [Civil Code] Section 3294 subdivision b, requires that the wrongful act giving rise to the exemplary damages be committed by 'an officer, director, or managing agent." (White v. Ultramar, Inc. (1999) 21 Cal.4th 563, 572. The complaint pleads that Dwight Gorall was Netwise's founder and CEO. (Id. at 3:13-14.) Motion denied.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Julius Balentine, CSR#13859, Email: juliusbalentinecsr@gmail.com, Tel:714.471.2597. (302/RBU)
